intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) appears empty/soil only. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling seedling/cutting. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor and a single leaf specimen. The registry is largely non-representative of current biological presence.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains; appears stable but isolated.
plant_audit.P4: Single green stem/sprout present, likely a propagation attempt or remnant.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in P4; electronic component (IC chip) observed on desk surface in early frames, removed by current state.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-3: Stable; P4 shows a small green sprout.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-1: Progressive loss of structural integrity in P4; the sprout appears to be wilting or collapsing.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The P4 sprout has lost its primary leaf structure, leaving only a thin, pale stem.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. P1 and P2 are effectively dormant or dead. P3 is the only stable element. P4 is experiencing rapid senescence or failure of the propagation attempt.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment or pH adjustment. The electronic component (IC chip) in early frames is a non-botanical anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently failing. The transition from the earliest image to the current state shows a clear degradation of the P4 specimen, which has lost its leaf surface area. The desk environment remains static, but the biological components are not thriving, likely due to the lack of sufficient light or improper substrate conditions for the specific species.
